claims.check: The cited exact-head sources support default boundary ownership for the managed context row and current ownership for sdk.session

session.ts:1229-1241 declares sdk.session ownership current
session-kernel.test.ts:206-215 resolves session before executing run

claims.check: The inspector sampler accounts for predeclared 1 KiB and 2 KiB allocation controls
claims.threshold: Controlled byte accounting with accepted zero noise and proportionality rules
claims.value: zero=112 bytes; 1 KiB control=269 bytes per iteration; 2 KiB control=501.5 bytes per iteration; ratio=1.8643122676579926
evidence: The replay exactly reproduced 112, 17216, and 32096 attributed bytes for 0, 64, and 128 retained controls.
The method does not account for the known 1024 and 2048 byte TypedArray backing allocations.

evidence: baseline=0
negativeAfterClose=0
positiveAfterClose=1
Node warned that v8.queryObjects is experimental; Node 26 pin and the 0/0/1 controls remain required

claims.check: The scope.run lifecycle row returns 42 and closes exactly one owned resource
evidence: ownedScopeRun={result:42,created:1,closed:1,accepted:true}

claims.check: The managed ctx.exec row represents default boundary ownership and reuses one resource until ctx.close
evidence: The probe declares ownership=current, so each ctx.exec child creates and closes its own resource.
The exact-head source-backed representative row requires default boundary ownership.

claims.check: The session row verifies completed status and work events on the same current-owned session runtime used by session.run
claims.threshold: result=42, status=completed, work.started and work.completed present, accepted=true
evidence: sdk.session is current-owned.
Resolving sdk.session after session.run creates or observes a different current runtime from the run child.
The exact-head session test resolves sdk.session on the managed context before executing run.
